remember: Students will be able to recall the purpose and preparation of a Cash Flow Statement.
Students will be able to identify the different types of cash flow statements.
Students will be able to explain the components of a Cash Flow Statement.
understand: Students will be able to understand the difference between operating, investing, and financing activities.
Students will be able to comprehend the impact of cash flow on business operations and decision-making.
Students will be able to appreciate the role of Cash Flow Statement in assessing a company's liquidity position.
apply: Students will be able to prepare a Cash Flow Statement from a given trial balance and other information.
Students will be able to analyze and interpret a Cash Flow Statement to evaluate a company's cash management.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the relationship between cash flow and profitability.
Students will be able to evaluate the impact of cash flow on a company's ability to pay its debts.

ncert_objectives: To understand the concept of cash flow statement and its importance in business decision-making.
To prepare a cash flow statement from a given trial balance and other information.
To analyze and interpret a cash flow statement to evaluate a company's cash management.
board_exam_objectives: To prepare a cash flow statement for a given scenario.
To analyze the cash flow statement to evaluate a company's liquidity position and cash management.
To identify the strengths and weaknesses of a company's cash management based on the cash flow statement.
